title: ipomoea baccata
intro: Ipomoea baccata, also known as the Himalayan morning glory, is a perennial vine that belongs to the Convolvulaceae family of flowering plants. Traditionally, Ipomoea baccata has been used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and pain, aiding in digestion, and supporting respiratory health. Ipomoea baccata can be prepared as a herbal tea, decoction, or powder, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
